definition:
1. bringing to an end , τελεσφόρον εἰς ἐνιαυτόν for a year completing its round , for a complete  year, (Homer); τελεσφόροι ἀραί, εὐχαί tending to accomplishment , (Aeschulus Tragicus); φάσματα δὸς τελεσφόρα grant accomplishment  to the visions, (Sophocles Tragicus); τ. χάριν δοῦναι to grant the favour of fulfilment , (Sophocles Tragicus) 
2. accomplishing one's purpose , Μοῖρα (Aeschulus Tragicus); Δίκη (Sophocles Tragicus); πεσεῖν ἐς τὸ μὴ τελεσφόρον to fall powerless to the ground, (Aeschulus Tragicus) 
3. bringing fruit to perfection , δένδρον (Plutarch): having the ordering  of a thing, with genitive, (Aeschulus Tragicus) (ML)
